title: सीता हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सीता हृदयम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
ध्यायामि जानकीं सीतां रामवामाङ्कसंस्थिताम्।
पद्मपत्रायताक्षीं तां सर्वलक्षणसंयुताम्॥

॥ हृदयम् ॥
सीते वैदेहि वामोरु रामपत्नि नमोऽस्तु ते।
जनकात्मजे विश्वजननि मैथिलि परमेश्वरि॥ १॥

त्वमेव श्रीर्महालक्ष्मीः त्वमेव विजयप्रदा।
त्वमेव धर्मपत्नी च त्वमेव भुवनेश्वरी॥ २॥

अशोकवनवासा त्वं त्रिजटाप्रियदर्शना।
हनूमत्सन्देशहर्षा रामनामजपप्रिया॥ ३॥

भूमिजा भूमिकन्या च सीतावनमहोत्सवा।
अयोध्याराज्ञी विदेहा मिथिलापुरवासिनी॥ ४॥

लवकुशजननी देवी पातिव्रत्यव्रताधिका।
अग्निपरीक्षासंशुद्धा सत्यशीला पतिव्रता॥ ५॥

शीलसौभाग्यसम्पन्ना सर्वसौन्दर्यसुन्दरी।
रामहृदयनिवासा रामप्राणप्रिया सदा॥ ६॥

सीताहृदयमाख्यानं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
पातिव्रत्यफलं तस्य सौभाग्यं च प्रजायते॥ ७॥

रामसीतासमेतस्य ध्यानात्पापं प्रणश्यति।
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षांश्च लभते नात्र संशयः॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री सीता हृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sita Hridayam ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Dhyaayami Janakim Sitam Rama-vamanka-samsthitam.
Padma-patrayatakshim Tam Sarva-lakshana-samyutam.

|| Hridayam ||
Site Vaidehi Vamoru Ramapatni Namostu Te.
Janaka-atmaje Vishwajanani Maithili Parameshwari. || 1 ||

Tvameva Shrir-Mahalakshmih Tvameva Vijayaprada.
Tvameva Dharmapatni Cha Tvameva Bhuvaneshwari. || 2 ||

Ashoka-vana-vasa Tvam Trijata-priya-darshana.
Hanumat-sandesha-harsha Rama-nama-japa-priya. || 3 ||

Bhumija Bhumikanya Cha Sitavana-mahotsava.
Ayodhya-rajni Videha Mithila-pura-vasini. || 4 ||

Lava-kusha-janani Devi Pativratya-vratadhika.
Agni-pariksha-samshudha Satya-shila Pativrata. || 5 ||

Shila-saubhagya-sampanna Sarva-saundarya-sundari.
Rama-hridaya-nivasa Rama-prana-priya Sada. || 6 ||

Sita-hridayam-aakhyanam Yah Pathed Bhakti-samyutah.
Pativratya-phalam Tasya Saubhagyam Cha Prajayate. || 7 ||

Rama-Sita-sametasya Dhyanat Papam Pranashyati.
Dharmartha-kama-mokshamsh-cha Labhate Natra Samshayah. || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Sita Hridayam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: सीता हृदयम् में माता सीता (जानकी, वैदेही, मैथिली) के दिव्य स्वरूप और गुणों का वर्णन है। सीता जी श्री, महालक्ष्मी, विजयप्रदा, धर्मपत्नी और भुवनेश्वरी हैं। अशोकवन में रहकर भी रामनाम जपती रहीं, अग्निपरीक्षा से शुद्ध सिद्ध हुईं, पातिव्रत्य की सर्वोच्च आदर्श हैं। जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है उसे सौभाग्य और पातिव्रत्य फल प्राप्त होता है तथा धर्म, अर्थ, काम, मोक्ष — चतुर्विध पुरुषार्थ की प्राप्ति होती है।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of pativratya (marital devotion) and saubhagya
Invokes the four purusharthas - dharma, artha, kama, moksha
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins through meditation on rama-sita
Especially beneficial for married women and families
Traditionally associated with divine grace of mother sita into the household
description: Dedicated to Goddess Sita, the Sita Hridayam (सीता हृदयम्) is a heart-essence prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Ananda Ramayana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to bring divine grace of Mother Sita into the household and destroy all sins through meditation on Rama-Sita. It is also said to bestow pativratya (marital devotion) and saubhagya. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Friday (Shukravar) and during Vivah Panchami and Ram Navami. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Sita Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Sita Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Sacred hymn to Goddess Sita for marital bliss and divine blessings.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sita Hridayam and where does it come from?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sita Hridayam (सीता हृदयम्) is a heart-essence (hridaya) Sanskrit hymn to Goddess Sita Devi, preserved in the Ananda Ramayana — a classical Ramayana retelling traditionally attributed to Maharshi Valmiki himself (though scholars place its current redaction in the medieval period). The composition opens with a Dhyana-shloka meditating on Sita seated on Rama's left lap with lotus-petal eyes and all auspicious lakshanas, and proceeds through eight verses that successively invoke Sita as Vaidehi, Vamoru, Rama-patni, Janaka-atmaja, Vishva-janani, Maithili, Parameshvari (verse 1); identify her with Shri, Mahalakshmi, Vijaya-prada, Dharma-patni and Bhuvaneshvari (verse 2); recall her Ashoka-vana, Trijata, Hanumat-sandesha, Rama-nama-japa episodes (verse 3); name her many divine epithets (verse 4); celebrate her agni-pariksha and pativratya (verse 5); declare her as Rama-hridaya-nivasa (verse 6); and conclude with the famous chaturvidha-purushartha (dharma-artha-kama-moksha) phalashruti. (Source: Ananda Ramayana stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Sita Hridayam different from Sita Ashtakam and Sita Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are Sanskrit Sita hymns but differ in source-text and ritual emphasis. The Sita Hridayam is from the Ananda Ramayana and is heart-essence-oriented (hridaya genre) — opening with a dhyana, focused on Sita as Lakshmi-Bhuvaneshvari and on her residence in Rama's heart, with a phalashruti specifically promising the four purusharthas (dharma, artha, kama, moksha). The Sita Ashtakam is an eight-verse biographical meditation tracing Sita's life from birth to return-to-earth, with phalashruti promising sarva-saubhagya. The Sita Stotram is a ten-verse Valmiki Ramayana composition focused on Sita's sacred names, with phalashruti emphasising progeny and pativrata grace. Together the three form the complete classical Sita liturgical set: Stotram for stuti, Ashtakam for biographical meditation, Hridayam for dhyana and chaturvidha-purushartha. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana; Ananda Ramayana.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the chaturvidha purusharthas promised in the Sita Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Sita Hridayam (verse 8) declares: Rama-Sita-sametasya dhyanat papam pranashyati, Dharmartha-kama-mokshamsh-cha labhate natra samshayah — by meditating on Rama-Sita together, all sins are destroyed and the chaturvidha-purushartha (the four ultimate goals of human life) are attained without doubt. The four purusharthas are: (1) Dharma — righteous conduct, the foundation of a good life; (2) Artha — prosperity, material well-being acquired through righteous means; (3) Kama — fulfilment of legitimate desires within dharma; (4) Moksha — final liberation from the cycle of birth and death. To promise all four through a single hymn is the highest possible promise of any stotra — equivalent to the Bhagavad-Gita's parallel teaching that those who surrender obtain all the goals of life. The Hridayam is therefore considered a complete spiritual path. (Source: Sita Hridayam phalashruti verse 8.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Sita Hridayam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sita Hridayam is most powerful on Friday (Shukravar — sacred to Lakshmi-Sita), and the major festival days are: Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla Panchami — the celestial wedding day of Sita-Rama at Janakpur, the most important day for this hymn), Sita Navami / Janaki Navami (Vaishakha Shukla Navami — Sita's appearance day), Rama Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami — Rama's appearance day), Akshaya Tritiya, Diwali (Sita-Rama's return to Ayodhya), and during Chaitra and Ashvina Navaratris. Married women traditionally recite it daily before the morning lamp for saubhagya, marital harmony and household auspiciousness. Couples seeking the four purusharthas — especially those balancing material and spiritual goals — recite it together. The dhyana-shloka opening makes it particularly suitable for meditation and ekagra-bhakti. (Sources: Drikpanchang Sita Navami Vivah Panchami; Ananda Ramayana parayana custom.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Sita called Rama-hridaya-nivasa in this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 6 of the Sita Hridayam declares Sita as Rama-hridaya-nivasa — the one who resides eternally in Rama's heart — and Rama-prana-priya sada — ever-beloved as Rama's very life-breath. This is one of the most theologically important declarations in the Sita corpus. It means that Sita is not merely Rama's external consort but his very inner essence — the Hladini-Shakti of Vishnu-Rama, inseparable from him as fragrance from a flower. In the Sita Upanishad and Sri Vaishnava theology, this relationship is called nitya-yoga — eternal union — and is the model for the jiva's relationship with Paramatma. Just as Sita resides in Rama's heart, the devotee's own heart becomes the abode of the divine when filled with Sita-bhava (the devotional mood of total surrender). The Hridayam's name itself (heart-essence) reflects this teaching. (Sources: Sita Upanishad; Sri Vaishnava Acharya commentaries; Adhyatma Ramayana.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily recitation of Sita Hridayam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sita Hridayam grants a uniquely complete set of blessings: (1) pativratya-phala — the merit of supreme marital devotion, (2) saubhagya — the boon of remaining married for life with the husband's long life and well-being, (3) papa-nasha — destruction of sins through meditation on Rama-Sita together, (4) chaturvidha-purushartha — attainment of dharma, artha, kama and moksha (the four ultimate goals of human life), (5) divine grace of Mother Sita (jagan-mata) entering the household, (6) marital harmony and family prosperity, (7) santana-sukha — the joy of children. The phalashruti is unique in promising both the worldly fruits (artha, kama, saubhagya) and the supreme spiritual fruit (moksha) — making this hymn equally suitable for grihasthas (householders) and mumukshus (seekers of liberation). (Sources: Sita Hridayam phalashruti; Ananda Ramayana.)
